subject: [For Review] March CB Quarterly Management Meeting

content.answer:
Hi Ian, Jianghong,

Please find attached version 1 deck for the upcoming Quarterly CB
Management Meeting on 31st Mar, Tuesday, for review.

Work-in-progress (WIP) parts will be completed by next iteration, tomorrow,
27th Mar.

Let me know for any questions.

-- 
*Jason YU*
*Regional CB | MY & SG CB*
*Mobile*: +65 90284825
*Email*: jason.yu@shopee.com

thanks, in general let's avoid long winded updates that are too
tactical/bottom up and aim to be concise + sharp + strategic with CF/TP

@Faye Yan <faye.yan@shopee.com> @Jason Yu <jason.yu@shopee.com> as
discussed

   - Currently the sob/yoy growth has way too much clutter:
      - Pls refer to the sg deck with CF/TP. To be concise and sharp about
      key msgs. Pls combine cb and cnls together and dont leave out kr/jp
      - Can summarize the tables into quarterly instead of monthly since
      this meeting with cf is quarterly
      - Pls focus on adg sob and move ado sob into appendix
      - Pls have 1 slide that clearly states that we are outperforming
      platform in terms of adg sob against tts
      - Pls have 1 slide that clearly states that we are on track to our
      YoY target
   - For fbs,
      - Pls combine cb and cnls into 1, focusing on the key number of cb +
      cnls fbs ado%. Currently way too confusing and way too much clutter
      - Pls have 1 slide that clearly states that our fbs ado% is growing
   - SLS/War impact/Automation
      - The summary of the pnl impact should be the 1st slide and concise
      - Pls also ensure alignment with reg fpna and local markets about
      what’s budgeted for what we show to CF/TP. Currently quite alot of
      confusion with what each local market budgets vs regional budget
      - Any update on automation
   - Temu: any development on market expansion or pushing further in SEA?
   If not lets make it clear as that's quite a important thing to track from
   overall org pov
   - The rest can move to a separate deck as update deck


--

*Chua Kel Jin*

Mobile: +65 8511 5071

Address: 5 Science Park Drive, Shopee Building, Singapore 118265

Please see my comments in the PDF file.

For the war impact, I'll talk with the team offline

Hi Keljin/Jianghong,

We have further sharpened the deck, please find response to your feedback
below and V2 of the completed decks attached.

Jianghong’s Feedback

Slide 3 -  1) SHP VN 3PF CMS increase by ~2.5% from Jan  - What is CMS and
what is the message we want to deliver here?

Slide 3 - 3) higher LFF ADO pene% of TTS compared with SHP (TTS ~64% vs SHP
~42% Think this one is not so important?

(Slide is simplified and details will be given in case CF asks)

CMS stands for commission rate.

The message CNCB team wants to deliver is that:

For CNCB ADO SOB, from Dec’25 to Mar’26, SHP VN 3PF actual commission take
rate increased by ~5% (from ~5.3% to 10.1%), primarily due to a commission
fee increase by ~2% starting from Jan’25, coupled with commission fee
waivers for some new 3PF sellers ended in Feb / Mar. This could pose
potential pressure for VN 3PF ADO growth.

In contrast, TTS shows a higher LFF ADO pene% in VN compared with SHP (64%
vs 42%), thus TTS’s robust LFF growth can largely drive the overall CB
topline growth (updated in slides)

However, looking into ADG SOB, VN ADG SOB QoQ dropped by -0.16x,
underperforming the platform by -0.15x. This is primarily due to quick TTS
CNCB ramp-up (ADG +29% QoQ) by aggressively expanding LFF sellers
specialized in large & bulky categories with higher ABS

Slide 3 - between TTS CNCB sellers and TTS platform (~3–4 p.p.) - What is
the root cause? Category mix?

The root cause of CIR% gap: TTS strategic PRM support on PHCB to gain
market share

   -

   As PH remains the only SEA market other than SG with SHP/TTS CB ADG SOB
   above 1.0x, TTS deploys strategic PRM, especially heavy logistic PRM, on
   PHCB to gain market share

The category mix remains broadly consistent between PH local and CB, with
nuances in FMCG and EL. (Note: local has slightly higher FMCG presence with
a lean EL, while CB features a more balanced split)

Slide 8 -  +17% below platform growth,  the

content.question:
Thanks.



Slide 8

   - For CB, can breakdown into SLS and LFF?

17% yoy is not very good
   - If we add CNLS into total CB, how will the YOY growth be like?

Slide 12

   - We fell behind for MY and PH?
I thought we were always in front?

Others

   - There is an initiative to acquire TTS only sellers via 3PFs?

Can we
   bring it up, and where are we on it?

Slide 16

   - Does this take into account LFF % increasing over time?

As LFF
   increases, the impact on SLS should by right decline?

Slide 17

   - How do I reconcile the numbers here with the 48M on Slide 16?

Others

   - Any content on our WH automation that we should like to bring up?
- What automation are we testing right now?
CPO impact?

How we are
   bringing these automation locally
   - Can bring SCS + Lovito content from update to discussion deck



*From:* Jason Yu <jason.yu@shopee.com>
*Sent:* Saturday, 28 March 2026 5:27 pm
*To:* Jianghong Liu <jianghong.liu@shopee.com>
*Cc:* Keljin Chua <keljin.chua@shopee.com>; Ian Ho <hoi@sea.com>; Amanda
Eng <amanda.eng@shopee.com>; Haenle Suh <haenle.suh@shopee.com>; Ray Wang <
ray.wang01@shopee.com>; Shuning Wang <shuning.wang@shopee.com>; Cuiwei Sun <
cuiwei.sun@shopee.com>; Faye Yan <faye.yan@shopee.com>; Max Goh Shi Long <
max.gohsl@shopee.com>; Angela Hsu Yi-Han <angela.hsu@shopee.com>; Lu Lin <
lu.lin@shopee.com>; Siqin Liu <siqin.liu@lovito.com>; Roy Lv <
roy.lv@shopee.com>; Di Zhang <di.zhangd@shopee.com>; Elaine Wang <
elaine.wang@shopee.com>; kexin hong <kexin.hong@shopee.com>; Leo Chen <
leo.chenyh@shopee.com>; Ruofei Wang <phyllis.wang@shopee.com>; Sophie Peng <
sophie.peng@shopee.com>
*Subject:* Re: [For Review] March CB Quarterly Management Meeting



Hi Keljin/Jianghong,



We have further sharpened the deck, please find response to your feedback
below and V2 of the completed decks attached.



*Jianghong’s Feedback*

Slide 3 -  1) SHP VN 3PF CMS increase by ~2.5% from Jan  - What is CMS and
what is the message we want to deliver here?

Slide 3 - 3) higher LFF ADO pene% of TTS compared with SHP (TTS ~64% vs SHP
~42% Think this one is not so important?

(Slide is simplified and details will be given in case CF asks)

CMS stands for commission rate.

The message CNCB team wants to deliver is that:

For CNCB ADO SOB, from Dec’25 to Mar’26, SHP VN 3PF actual commission take
rate increased by ~5% (from ~5.3% to 10.1%), primarily due to a commission
fee increase by ~2% starting from Jan’25, coupled with commission fee
waivers for some new 3PF sellers ended in Feb / Mar. This could pose
potential pressure for VN 3PF ADO growth.

In contrast, TTS shows a higher LFF ADO pene% in VN compared with SHP (64%
vs 42%), thus TTS’s robust LFF growth can largely drive the overall CB
topline growth (updated in slides)

However, looking into ADG SOB, VN ADG SOB QoQ dropped by -0.16x,
underperforming the platform by -0.15x. This is primarily due to quick TTS
CNCB ramp-up (ADG +29% QoQ) by aggressively expanding LFF sellers
specialized in large & bulky categories with higher ABS



Slide 3 - between TTS CNCB sellers and TTS platform (~3–4 p.p.) - What is
the root cause?
Category mix?

The root cause of CIR% gap: TTS strategic PRM support on PHCB to gain
market share

   - As PH remains the only SEA market other than SG with SHP/TTS CB ADG
   SOB above 1.0x, TTS deploys strategic PRM, especially heavy logistic PRM,
   on PHCB to gain market share

The category mix remains broadly consistent between PH local and CB, with
nuances in FMCG and EL. (Note: local has slightly higher FMCG presence with
a lean EL, while CB features a more balanced split)



Slide 8 -  +17% below platform growth,  there should be a comma here,
otherwise the meaning is very different

Have updated the title.

Slide 8 - expansion and automation This one refers to the FBS warehouse
expansion and automation cost allocated to CB?

Yes.



Slide 20 (Now moved to For Update)- FBS Free Trial Program - Can remind me
whether we still have a "free trial" program?

Yes. We are still running Free Trial for all CB FBS markets, incl. SG, MY,
TH, PH, aligned with local FBS programs as well.



Slide 20  - On this slide, you better list down a comparison table between
Jet fuel volatility compared to vehicle fuel (SPX). 1) Higher 2) Delayed

We have added into the updated slides.



Slide 21  - To mark where we are now on the chart

We have added into the updated slides.

Slide 22 - indicative of diesel prices in SEA - You want to compare to SPX
fuel cost here, right?

Yes we want to leverage the chart to highlight jet fuel's greater price
volatility vs diesel (SPX fuel cost).



Slide 56 - Included 新拼姆



*Keljin’s Feedback*

   - Currently the sob/yoy growth has way too much clutter:

·       Pls refer to the sg deck with CF/TP. To be concise and sharp about
key msgs. Pls combine cb and cnls together and dont leave out kr/jp

·       Can summarize the tables into quarterly instead of monthly since
this meeting with cf is quarterly

·       Pls focus on adg sob and move ado sob into appendix

·       Pls have 1 slide that clearly states that we are outperforming
platform in terms of adg sob against tts

·       Pls have 1 slide that clearly states that we are on track to our
YoY target

For FY26 Topline, we have combined the CB and CNLS and trimmed insights.
For SOB we have trimmed to show ADG by Quarter and sharpen the insights.
ADO SOB is placed as a back up slide.

   - For fbs,

·       Pls combine cb and cnls into 1, focusing on the key number of cb +
cnls fbs ado%. Currently way too confusing and way too much clutter

·       Pls have 1 slide that clearly states that our fbs ado% is growing

Have combined and shortened the slides, the rest moved to For Updates

   - SLS/War impact/Automation


   - The summary of the pnl impact should be the 1st slide and concise

Noted, we are starting the session with the P&L impact.

·       Pls also ensure alignment with reg fpna and local markets about
what’s budgeted for what we show to CF/TP. Currently quite alot of
confusion with what each local market budgets vs regional budget

Aligned with Faye on the PnL numbers

·       Any update on automation

Added automation slides to the deck.

   - Temu: any development on market expansion or pushing further in SEA?

content.answer:
Hi Ian,

Please see replies below in blue and updated slides attached, let me know
if any other questions.

Additionally, have replaced SOB with Slide 3 which combines view of CB/CNLS
to show total SOB improvement vs platform, moved CB/CNLS split into
appendix.

Slide 8 (Now Slide 5)

   -

   For CB, can breakdown into SLS and LFF? 17% yoy is not very good

Added in deck, can also see the breakdown in the table below. Table may
trigger comparison questions (e.g., why SLS MP PC2 is negative, why LFF YoY
PC2% fell). A cleaner narrative for ADG: +17% YoY, despite not strong, it’s
straightforward to explain,

   -

   with most of the gap driven by TH/VN downturns (platform +35%/+25% vs.
   CB +16%/0%)
   -

   Also note on deck that Q4’26 vs Q4’25 is +24%, in line with the platform
   +26% for the same period, by then, the anti‑import policies were already in
   effect in Q4’25

[image: image.png]



   -

   If we add CNLS into total CB, how will the YOY growth be like?

+22%, can see 2nd last row in the deck table


Slide 12 (Now Slide 11)

   -

   We fell behind for MY and PH? I thought we were always in front?

MY and PH was on par with TTS in Dec’25 but fell slightly behind from
Jan’26 to Feb’26, mainly due to 1) TTS didn’t increase base comm while SHP
mandatory fee increase significantly from Jan (MY CB FBS base comm
increased by 4% + 5% new mandatory ads to all CN sellers; PHCB FBS base
comm increased by 1.5%) and 2) TTS tends to spike more in Jan/Feb, slowing
down in later months


To catch up by Jun, we are accelerating FBS growth by setting more
aggressive targets for MY/PH and optimizing our existing FBS penetration
program. We will also grow total LFF seller base by running TTS 3PF seller
acquisition program.


Others

   -

   There is an initiative to acquire TTS only sellers via 3PFs? Can we
   bring it up, and where are we on it?


Yes, we’ve added this as slide 12
